concerns.text: Task T013 implements a halt condition for N < 30 referencing FR-002. However, the Plan (Phase 1) and scientific rigor requirements mandate a hard stop at N < 130. T013 silently weakens the power constraint defined in the Plan, creating a logical contradiction where the task satisfies the outdated Spec but violates the updated Plan's scientific validity.

Task T014 implements a halt for N < 130 but references 'per plan.md' while simultaneously co-existing with T013 (N < 30). This creates a split implementation of the same FR (FR-002) where the deliverable is ambiguous: does the system halt at 30 or 130? The task list fails to consolidate the power check into a single, Plan-compliant logic block.

Task T028 implements the robustness check unconditionally. While this aligns with the Plan's update to remove the r > 0.3 condition, it directly contradicts the Spec's FR-006 and US-3 acceptance criteria which mandate the check only runs if correlation > 0.3. The task silently relaxes the Spec's conditional logic without explicit 'staged' documentation, violating the Spec's defined scope.

Task T014 introduces a warning band for 130 <= N < 200. The Spec's Edge Cases and FR-002 only define thresholds at N < 30 and 30 <= N < 100. This task introduces a new acceptance criterion (the 130-200 warning) not present in the Spec, effectively altering the system's defined behavior without a corresponding Spec update.
